Article.ArticleTitle: Quantitative Comparison of Photoprotective Effects of Tinted Versus Nontinted Sunscreens With Equivalent Formulations and Sun Protection Factor in Middle-Eastern Women With Fitzpatrick Skin Types II to IV: A Single-Blinded Randomized Clinical Trial.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Center for Research and Training in Skin Diseases and Leprosy, Tehran University of Medical Sciences, Tehran, Iran.

Reference.Citation: B. Li, H. Mei, Z. Zhou, J. Yang, Y. Zhang, and F. Qi, “The Main Causes and Corresponding Solutions of Skin Pigmentation in the Body,” Journal of Dermatologic Science and Cosmetic Technology 1, no. 2 (2024): 100020.
A. S. Farberg, A. M. Glazer, A. C. Rigel, R. White, and D. S. Rigel, “Dermatologists' Perceptions, Recommendations, and Use of Sunscreen,” JAMA Dermatology 153, no. 1 (2017): 99–101.

J. S. Lee, J. Ha, K. Shin, H. Kim, and S. Cho, “Different Cosmetic Habits Can Affect the Biophysical Profile of Facial Skin: A Study of Korean and Chinese Women,” Annals of Dermatology 31, no. 2 (2019): 175–185.
A. Gonzalez‐Bravo, T. Montero‐Vilchez, S. Arias‐Santiago, and A. Buendia‐Eisman, “The Effect of Sunscreens on the Skin Barrier,” Life (Raleigh) 12, no. 12 (2022): 2083.
